_meta.purpose: execute a lightweight fix in a single human-copiloted session: fluid, low overhead, code-focused. Nothing in agents/fixes/ during execution; retrospective documentation only at closure.

operating_model.branch_creation: the human creates the branch and tells the fixer its name
operating_model.session_state: lives in the branch's commits; nothing in agents/fixes/ while the session is open
operating_model.bundling: accumulating adjacent fixes on the same branch is allowed, without opening a new branch or editing md at every commit
operating_model.closure: the human explicitly signals closure; the fixer then creates the retrospective fix.md and opens the PR (or awaits human authorization)

preflight: read the governance manifest at the root, agents/governance/developer-guidelines.md (§5 rules and review-checklist) and agents/governance/verification-suite.md (architectural gates) before any write
confirm the active branch: if ambiguous, ask the human before any write
read the branch's git log to understand what has been done (recovery)
read methodology.md §1.5 (project stage) and §8 (fix lane rules)
do not run the feature reconciliation preflight; do not validate status.yaml invariants; do not resolve the skill compliance gate
writable_files: product files needed for the correction (no pre-derived list; the human copilots and interrupts if scope drifts)
forbidden_actions: create a branch (action reserved for the human)
create agents/fixes/<branch-suffix>/ or fix.md before the human closure signal
create status.yaml or a revisions/ folder in the fix lane
open a PR without explicit human authorization
make a decision listed in stop_and_ask_gates without asking the human
run a database reset, table drop or equivalent discard without an explicit prior warning in chat (methodology.md §1.5)
emit intermediate updates outside the events allowed by transitions.json > communication_contract.allowed_chat_events
stop_and_ask_gates: database migration (any change to persisted schema)
auth, session, token, permission, crypto, secrets
breaking a public contract consumed by another process (request/response, event payload, persisted format)
adding, removing, or major-bumping a dependency
root-cause ambiguity (the symptom admits 2+ plausible causes with different fixes)
golden_rule: if the decision is hard to revert without a rebase or without coordinating with another system: ask. if a local revert solves it: decide and narrate in <=1 line in chat
rules: the human is copiloting in real time (logs, application); speed is the priority
do not create speculative abstraction or broad refactors; solve the symptom with the smallest possible delta
reversible decisions follow 'narrate and continue' (chat <=1 line) per the golden_rule
decisions in stop_and_ask_gates require a direct question to the human before any write
before instructing a DB reset or equivalent destructive action, warn the human and await confirmation
respect commits marked (human); (human) commits are part of the bundle but do not count as agent evidence
if the scope starts becoming a broad refactor or non-trivial design, signal the human and offer promotion to feature (methodology.md §8.4)
skills are an optional resource; consult agents/skills/<name>.md by technical need, without a gate

closure_protocol.trigger: explicit human closure signal for the bundle (e.g., 'close fix', 'you can wrap up', equivalent)
closure_protocol.branch_suffix_resolution: extract the part after 'fix/' from the branch name; on a name conflict or incompatibility, ask the human before creating the folder
steps: create agents/fixes/<branch-suffix>/fix.md from the lightweight-fix template
fill in Objective (1-3 lines), Files Touched (lean list) and Evidence (relevant branch commits + test commands executed, if any)
commit fix.md on the branch itself
ask the human in a single objective message whether to open the PR or whether there are further adjustments
if authorized: open the PR using fix.md + git log as the description base; close the session

closing_actions: commit and push the versioned changes

handoff.context_compaction: resume in the next session from the branch's git log (no status.yaml)
handoff.blocker_or_ambiguity: escalate to the human in a single objective message

closing_output: Single dense message (<=5 lines): active branch, summary of what was fixed, PR opened/awaiting authorization, or the blocker preventing closure.
